• Show All Code
  • Hide All Code
  • Download Rmd

Report Data Cleaning

Cristin Young

2026-05-07

Project Overview

This notebook prepares CYBHI EBP/CDEP program data for analysis and reporting. It ingests two raw exports from the eInsight clinical management platform, cleans and recodes each, joins them together, and writes three clean CSVs used by downstream analysis scripts.

Data sources (eInsight exports):

  • Discharge Report — One row per client assessment pair (pre/post). Contains intervention dates, pre/post measure names, and funding round.
  • Client Demographics — One row per client (ideally). Contains race, ethnicity, language, age, provider, and other client characteristics.

Outputs:

File Contents
demographic_data_clean.csv Deduplicated, recoded demographic data
discharge_data_clean.csv Cleaned discharge/assessment data
demographic_discharge_clean.csv Joined dataset for outcome analyses

Open Issues / Action Items

Setup

library(tidyverse)
library(janitor)
library(gt)
library(readxl)
library(openxlsx)

1. Read in Raw Data

Both files are pulled from the eInsight export folder in the shared Dropbox. The na argument ensures that common “missing” strings are treated as NA from the start. Column names are cleaned to snake_case via janitor::clean_names(). Date columns in the discharge data are stored as numeric by Excel and need explicit conversion.

cat("Unique demographic IDs:  ", n_distinct(demographic_data$client_system_id), "\n")

Unique demographic IDs:   53134

2. Clean Demographic Data

2.1 Remove Demo/Test Accounts

eInsight includes test provider and client accounts with “DEMO” in their IDs. These are not real clients and must be excluded before any analysis.

2.2 Identify and Handle Duplicate Client IDs

The demographics export should have one row per client, but some clients appear more than once (e.g., due to selecting multiple languages or data entry errors). We identify duplicates, export them for review, then drop them from the analysis dataset for now.

# Confirm: should now be TRUE (unique IDs)
length(unique(demographic_data_filtered$client_system_id)) == nrow(demographic_data_filtered)

[1] TRUE

2.3 Recode Race

Race is stored as a free-text, comma-separated field. We clean it in two passes:

  1. Strip non-informative responses (“Don’t Know”, “Prefer Not To Answer”, “Not Applicable”).
  2. Recode to a numeric summary:
    • NA — no valid response after stripping
    • 1 — exactly one valid race reported
    • 2 — two or more valid races reported (multiracial)
    • code_num assigns each single-race response a numeric code, or 88 for multiracial.

2.4 Recode Ethnicity

Same two-pass approach as race. Ethnicity is also stored as a comma-separated string. Non-informative values are removed and responses are recoded to numeric codes; 88 = 2+ ethnicities selected.

3. Clean Discharge Data

3.1 Remove Demo/Test Accounts

Same as demographics — filter out any rows where the provider or client ID contains “DEMO”.

# Should be FALSE — one client can have multiple assessment rows
length(unique(discharge_data_filtered$client_system_id)) == nrow(discharge_data_filtered)

[1] FALSE

3.2 Inspect Duplicate Client Records

Unlike demographics, it is expected for a client to appear more than once in the discharge report (e.g., multiple interventions or follow-ups). This chunk documents which columns vary across rows for the same client.

3.3 Parse Pre/Post Measure Names

The pre_measure_s and post_measure_s fields combine the time point (e.g., “Initial”, “Follow Up 1”) and assessment name (e.g., “SDQ”) into one string. We split these into separate columns so they can be matched and filtered independently.

A secondary trimmed name (first word only) is created because some providers abbreviate assessment names differently in the pre vs. post fields.

unique(discharge_data_filtered$post_measure_s)

 [1] NA                                                           
 [2] "NOMS Self-Report Follow Up 1"                               
 [3] "Pediatric Symptom Checklist (PSC-35) Follow Up 1"           
 [4] "PSS Follow up 1"                                            
 [5] "NOMS Child Follow Up 1"                                     
 [6] "CPSS-5 Follow up 1"                                         
 [7] "CPSS 5 - Interviewer Version Follow up 1"                   
 [8] "SDQ Parent (age 2-4) Follow up 1"

[10] "SDQ Parent or Teacher (age 4-10) Follow Up 1"

[34] "CATS-2 Caregiver Report (age 7-17) Follow Up 1"

[37] "CATS-2 Caregiver Report (age 7-17) Follow Up 2"             
[38] "YOQ 30.2 Self Report Follow up 2"                           
[39] "Strengths and Difficulties Questionnaire P11-17 Follow up 3"
[40] "SDQ Parent (age 11-17) Follow up 2"                         
[41] "YOQ 30.2 Self Report Follow up 3"                           
[42] "GAD-7 Follow up 4"                                          
[43] "CPSS-5 Follow up 5"                                         
[44] "CPSS-5 Follow up 6"                                         
[45] "CPSS-5 Follow up 7"                                         
[46] "CPSS-5 Follow up 11"                                        
[47] "CPSS-5 Follow up 12"                                        
[48] "GAD-7 Follow up 2"                                          
[49] "GAD-7 Follow up 3"                                          
[50] "GAD-7 Follow up 5"                                          
[51] "PHQ-9 Follow up 5"                                          
[52] "CATS - Caregiver Report (Ages 3-6) Follow Up 2"             
[53] "PHQ-9 Follow up 3"                                          
[54] "CPSS-5 Follow up 2"                                         
[55] "YOQ 30.2 Self Report Follow up 4"                           
[56] "NOMS Self-Report Follow Up 7"                               
[57] "SDQ Parent (age 2-4) Follow up 3"                           
[58] "NOMS Self-Report Follow Up 5"                               
[59] "NOMS Self-Report Follow Up 9"                               
[60] "NOMS Self-Report Follow Up 10"                              
[61] "NOMS Self-Report Follow Up 8"                               
[62] "NOMS Self-Report Follow Up 6"                               
[63] "Basis-24 Follow up 2"                                       
[64] "Basis-24 Follow up 1"                                       
[65] "PHQ-9 Follow up 4"

3.4 Fix Missing Funding Rounds

Some providers did not select a funding round in eInsight (“No Funding Round Selected”). Based on program records, we manually assign the correct round for known providers.

Provider ID Organization Correct Round
604 Monterey Health Dept 2
508 Children of the Rainbow 2
607 Youth for Change 1
573 Homeless Children’s Network 2
709 Placer County Office of Education 1

4. EDA — Clients with Multiple Interventions

Some clients appear in the discharge data more than once because they participated in more than one intervention (or had multiple follow-up time points). Understanding this structure is important before analysis, because rows are not independent.

4.1 Identify Clients with >1 Complete Intervention

“Complete” here means the row has both a pre AND a post measure (i.e., the client has a pre–post pair, not just a baseline or a lone follow-up).

cat("Clients with >1 complete pre-post pair:", n_distinct(dup_clients$client_system_id), "\n")

Clients with >1 complete pre-post pair: 685

4.2 Concurrent Interventions (Same Pre-Completion Date)

Clients who had two or more different assessments completed on the same date — indicating they were enrolled in multiple interventions simultaneously.

cat("Clients with concurrent interventions:", nrow(dup_clients_same_time), "\n")

Clients with concurrent interventions: 355

4.3 Sequential Interventions (Different Start Dates)

Clients who completed interventions at genuinely different time points — i.e., they started a new intervention after finishing a prior one.

cat("Total dup_clients:          ", n_distinct(dup_clients$client_system_id), "\n")

Total dup_clients:           685

5. Join Demographic and Discharge Data

Left join from discharge data onto demographics so we retain all assessment rows. Clients without a demographics record will have NA in demographic columns but remain in the dataset.

ID columns need to be the same type (character) before joining; they can come in as numeric from Excel.

6. Quick Counts

cat("Unique clients in joined dataset (outcomes):  ", n_distinct(all_data$client_system_id), "\n")

Unique clients in joined dataset (outcomes):   42706

7. Write Clean CSVs

cat("CSVs written successfully.\n")

CSVs written successfully.
